agents.title: AI Agent
agents.desc: Microkernel WASM. Each agent stands alone — set router, prompt, tools, and schedule via the Setting button on its card.
agents.upload_sub: Drop a .fwagent.zip — manifest is validated and files extract to ~/.flowork/agents/<id>.fwagent/.
agents.drop_label: Drag & drop, or click to choose .fwagent.zip
agents.drop_hint: Max 64 MiB. Zip must contain manifest.json and agent.wasm.
agents.list_empty: No agents installed yet. Upload a .fwagent.zip above.
agents.remove_confirm: Remove agent {id}? Folder ~/.flowork/agents/{id}.fwagent/ will be deleted.
agents.remove_confirm_full: Remove agent {id} permanently? Source folder + workspace + state.db will be deleted.
agents.prompt_lbl: System prompt — persona & doctrine when calling the router (isolated per agent).
agents.prompt_ph: You are Mr.Flow — an AI agent for the owner...
agents.schedule_sub: Per-agent recurring jobs. Cron format: `min hr dom mon dow`. Recurring or one-shot.
agents.schedule_task_ph: What the agent should do on trigger
agents.tools_sub: Capabilities the agent may use (isolated per agent). Tick = enabled.
agents.skills_sub: Reusable behaviours — procedures the agent knows (isolated). Trigger = when to fire, instructions = mini-prompt.
agents.skills_router_error: Router unreachable. Check Settings → LLM Router URL.
agents.slash_modal_sub: Type /command or click hint. Enter = run, Esc = close.
agents.tools_catalog_sub: Subscribe = appears in suggest pool + UI listing.
agents.settings_sub: API credentials & external IDs — fully isolated per agent. Each key stored in this agent's config.json; never leaks to other agents.
agents.settings_creds_sub: Add secrets/API keys/user IDs this agent needs. Example: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, GOOGLE_API_KEY, YOUTUBE_API_KEY, NOTION_TOKEN, OPENAI_API_KEY, etc.
agents.database_sub: Per-agent SQLite — fully isolated inside the workspace. Only this agent reads/writes. Agent creates its own schema via SQL.
agents.database_reset_confirm: Reset state.db for agent {id}? All SQLite data lost permanently.
agents.workspace_sub: Each agent has an isolated workspace, auto-mounted at `/workspace` on the guest side. Only this agent can read/write here.
agents.shared_sub: Shared folder for cross-agent collaboration. Agent must declare cap `fs:shared` in manifest to mount it.
agents.shared_off: This agent has no `fs:shared` capability — `/shared` is not mounted.
agents.save_ok: ✅ Saved. Hot-reload re-injects env into the agent.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Owner-level configuration — sign-in and API keys. AI agents stay fully isolated.
settings.account_sub: Update the password you use to sign in.
settings.logout_sub: Sign out of this browser session.
settings.keys_sub: Global API keys used by Flowork. Stored in flowork.db, injected as env vars. Values are masked.
settings.notify_sub: Owner-level Telegram for host alerts (scanner findings, watchdog). SEPARATE from AI agents — agents stay isolated/plug-and-play with their own channel.
settings.notify_chat_hint: Get your chat ID: message @userinfobot on Telegram. Can reuse the same bot token as your agent, or a separate notifier bot.
settings.keys_edit_hint: Editing — enter a new value, then Save.
settings.yt_sub: Connect a channel for automatic uploads.
settings.yt_guide_title: One-time setup — create an OAuth client in Google Console
settings.yt_guide_body:
1. console.cloud.google.com → create a project.
2. Enable: YouTube Data API v3 + YouTube Analytics API.
3. OAuth consent screen → External → add your email as a Test user.
4. Credentials → OAuth client ID → Desktop app → Download JSON.
Paste the JSON contents below.
settings.yt_paste_ph: Paste OAuth client JSON (Desktop app) here...
settings.yt_connect_hint: Browser opens → log in + allow → connected automatically. No copy-paste.
settings.yt_no_creds: No credential yet. Paste the OAuth client JSON above first.
settings.yt_open_auth: If the browser did not open, click here
settings.grd_desc: Auto-protects at every start (zero-config): records a fresh baseline and the sentinel guards the runtime. If a kernel file/cap is tampered, Flowork enters SAFE-MODE + alerts you. For OS-level immutability (binary can't be replaced at all), run `sudo flowork --arm` once.
settings.grd_arm_confirm: Record the current binary + kernel as the trusted baseline and start verifying every boot?
